title: Forkserver start method not available under MSYS2/MinGW64
body:
In the latest commit of Verilator (Commit ID 9d749841633f32c424f22acb37620f848ea111ce) running `make test` after a clean build under MSYS2 gives the error, `ValueError: forkserver start method not available`. This is due to MSYS2 not supporting UNIX `fork()` calls. Possibly add a catch for this and/or add Windows `spawn` support? Or am I doing something wrong here?
Here is the full traceback:
```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/JFlusche3180/verilator/test_regress/./driver.py", line 3019, in <module>
    run_them()
  File "/home/JFlusche3180/verilator/test_regress/./driver.py", line 2817, in run_them
    runner.wait_and_report()
  File "/home/JFlusche3180/verilator/test_regress/./driver.py", line 517, in wait_and_report
    more_now = forker.poll()
               ^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/home/JFlusche3180/verilator/test_regress/./driver.py", line 317, in poll
    self._run(process)
  File "/home/JFlusche3180/verilator/test_regress/./driver.py", line 354, in _run
    ctx = multiprocessing.get_context('forkserver')
          ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/usr/lib/python3.12/multiprocessing/context.py", line 243, in get_context
    return super().get_context(method)
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/usr/lib/python3.12/multiprocessing/context.py", line 194, in get_context
    ctx._check_available()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.12/multiprocessing/context.py", line 316, in _check_available
    raise ValueError('forkserver start method not available')
ValueError: forkserver start method not available
make: *** [Makefile:177: smoke-test] Error 1
```
